title: Exporting a WorkForm
excerpt: Hi all, is there a possibility to export a WorkForm layout to a PDF or Word document? I understand there are plenty of ways to manage the responses, however I have some clients that require offline and even manual copies…

posts.author: Michael_S (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, is there a possibility to export a WorkForm layout to a PDF or Word document? I understand there are plenty of ways to manage the responses, however I have some clients that require offline and even manual copies of some forms. Any advice would be super helpful.
TIA!

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello @Michael_S, My workaround for this has been to click print and select to PDF. Or you could use a free tool to print screen and paste into a Word Doc like “snipping tool”

posts.author: Michael_S (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Clare! I will progress with these until something ends up on the roadmap

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Michael_S

You can use the DocExport PDF Generator app to generate PDF documents from your WorkForm submissions.

When a form is submitted and a new item is created on your board, you can use that as a trigger for DocExport. It will automatically generate a PDF based on your customized design (or one of our starter templates) and save it to a Files column. You can also set it up to send the PDF automatically via email to the requester or anyone else.

We’ve created a step-by-step guide for this exact use case, including a video walkthrough: Convert form submissions to PDF with monday.com

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions.
